title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: Wednesday, May 13, 2026 - Playoff Action Heats Up!

items.headline: Mitchell Ties Playoff Record with Explosive Second Half!
items.summary: Donovan Mitchell erupted for 39 points in the second half of Game 4 on Monday, tying an NBA playoff record and fueling the Cleveland Cavaliers to a 112-103 victory over the Detroit Pistons. The series is now deadlocked at 2-2 heading back to Detroit.

items.headline: Wemby Wows in Game 5: Spurs Take 3-2 Lead!
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama delivered a monstrous performance with 27 points, 17 rebounds, 5 assists, and 3 blocks as the San Antonio Spurs routed the Minnesota Timberwolves 126-97 in Game 5 on Tuesday night. The Spurs now lead their Western Conference Semifinals series 3-2.

items.headline: Knicks, Thunder Punch Tickets to Conference Finals with Sweeps!
items.summary: The New York Knicks and Oklahoma City Thunder have both completed dominant sweeps in their respective conference semifinal series, becoming the first teams to advance to the Conference Finals.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Wolves to Take Series Lead
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama led the charge as the Spurs convincingly defeated the Timberwolves in Game 5, taking a crucial 3-2 lead in the Western Conference Semifinals.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell's Historic Outburst
items.statContext: Tied NBA playoff record for points in a half in Game 4 vs DET.

items.headline: Wemby's Playoff Masterclass
items.statContext: Led Spurs to Game 5 victory over MIN; third-youngest to post such a stat line in playoffs.

items.headline: Harden's 40th Playoff Double-Double
items.statContext: Crucial performance for the Cavaliers in their Game 4 win.

items.headline: Randle Delivers for Wolves
items.statContext: Strong effort for Minnesota in their Game 5 loss to the Spurs.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Playoff Punch
items.statContext: The Israeli forward made an All-Star leap this season, leading the Blazers to the playoffs with a 41-point play-in performance and later scoring 26 in a playoff game.

items.headline: Pistons Host Cavaliers for Pivotal Game 5 Tonight
items.summary: The Eastern Conference Semifinals continue tonight as the Detroit Pistons (DET) host the Cleveland Cavaliers (CLE) in Game 5. The series is tied 2-2, with tip-off scheduled for 8:00 PM ET on ESPN from Little Caesars Arena in Detroit.

items.headline: Playoff Picture: Series Updates
items.summary: With the Knicks and Thunder already through, the Cavaliers and Pistons are tied 2-2 in their Eastern Conference Semifinals, while the Spurs lead the Timberwolves 3-2 in the Western Conference.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade Rumors Swirl, East Preferred?
items.summary: Speculation continues around Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo, with reports suggesting he would prefer to remain in the Eastern Conference if a trade were to materialize. Teams like the Celtics, Lakers, and Warriors have been linked to the superstar.

items.headline: Sixers Trade Jared McCain to OKC for Draft Capital
items.summary: The Philadelphia 76ers have traded Jared McCain to the Oklahoma City Thunder in exchange for a 2026 first-round pick and three second-round picks. The move, approved by ownership, has drawn some criticism.

items.headline: Pistons Grapple with Injury Concerns Ahead of Game 5
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons face Game 5 with several key players listed as questionable, including Duncan Robinson (back), Caris LeVert (heel), and Kevin Huerter (adductor).
